content.full_text:
April 30, 1992

Honorable Ben Davidian, Chairman  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 "J" Street, Suite 800  
Sacramento, California 95804

Re: Use of Excess Campaign Funds

Dear Chairman Davidian:

We have been requested by Los Angeles County Supervisor Kenneth Hahn to seek the opinion of the Fair Political Practices Commission regarding the following factual situation:

After 40 years on the Board of Supervisors, Supervisor Hahn will retire when his term expires at the end of this year. He currently holds campaign funds previously raised which will not be needed for any future campaign purposes. Before he leaves office a retirement celebration will be held to honor Supervisor Hahn and provide him with the opportunity to thank those persons with whom he has worked during his long tenure with the County. He would like to use a portion of his excess campaign funds to help defray the expense of this celebration. If there are celebration costs not covered by such excess campaign funds, they will either be underwritten by individual sponsors or covered through an admission charge for the event. Gifts, if any, presented to Supervisor Hahn or his family at this celebration will be purchased with funds derived from the sources other than the excess campaign funds.

Government Code Section 89513(f)(2) allows the use of excess campaign funds to hold "an election victory celebration or similar campaign event." We believe that the described celebration would fall within the category of a "similar campaign event" as the term is used in this section. However, we would appreciate your opinion as to whether such an expenditure of excess campaign funds is permissible under the Political Reform Act.

Honorable Ben Davidian

In addition, we would also appreciate your opinion as to whether persons sponsoring the event or paying an admission charge to attend should be considered to have made a campaign contribution for which appropriate record keeping and reporting is necessary. We believe that it is clear from these circumstances that such a payment would not be for political purposes. Therefore, the payments should not be contributions under Section 82015.

If additional information is needed please feel free to contact me.

Very truly yours,

DE WITT W. CLINTON
County Counsel

By [signature]
STEVEN J. CARNEVALE
Principal Deputy County Counsel
Public Services Division

SJC:sc

cc: Supervisor Kenneth Hahn
